revisedPrompt:
Generate exactly one PNG image: a 64x64 px pixel-art terrain tileset sheet for a game, 4 rows x 4 columns, 16 tiles total, each tile exactly 16x16 px. Crisp SNES-inspired 16-bit pixel art, hard square pixels, no antialiasing blur, consistent bright mythical-Japan satoyama palette, warm golden-hour top-left light, hand-painted fantasy countryside feeling. Fully opaque PNG, no alpha. No visible grid lines, no labels, no tile IDs, no borders, no UI text, no watermark. Every tile fills its 16x16 cell edge-to-edge, stays contained in its cell, and keeps the same scale; neighboring cells should be tileable and cohesive without reading as repeated square stamps.

Subject: top-down terraced rice paddy / tanada autotile set, not coastline. Calm mirror-like paddy water reflecting blue sky and warm gold light, subtle ripple distortion, faint thin rows of young bright-green rice seedlings, tiny upper-left specular highlights. Earthen aze levee ridges are moist dark mud topped with bright green grass lips. For every edge/corner ridge, show this 3-part height formula in miniature: bright grass lip on top, vertical dark mud-and-moss face in the middle, soft contact shadow at the base. Organic curved borders only: no flat blue rectangles, no straight 90-degree corners, no dead-straight edge bands. Use painterly soft color ramps while preserving crisp pixel clusters, one-pixel dark outline where useful.

Strict layout:
Row 1, columns 1-4: four open paddy-water interior variations only, seamless mirror water plus rice rows, ripples, sky/gold reflections, no border.
Row 2, columns 1-4: water-to-aze edge tiles in order N, S, W, E. The aze ridge appears only on that side, water occupies the rest. The ridge line is irregularly curved or gently wavy at sub-tile scale, with green grass invading the water margin by a few pixels.
Row 3, columns 1-4: outer corner tiles in order NW, NE, SW, SE. Two aze ridges meet in an organically rounded corner, not a sharp right angle; soften with small grass tufts, reed tips, mud protrusions.
Row 4, columns 1-4 accent tiles: (1) grass and mud peninsula intruding deeply into water from one side, (2) dense reed cluster at paddy edge, (3) small muddy bald patch on aze with exposed dark soil and a tiny overflow trickle watermark, (4) lotus leaf and tiny frog on still water.

Material and style rules: world-map readability, broad regional color drift, natural transition blending, no hard rectangular bands. Bright cheerful palette, no dark gloomy mood, no modern objects, no weapons, no armor, no logos, no recognizable franchise characters or likenesses, no bird or companion creature, no letter emblems. If any unused empty pixels exist, fill them with solid #FF00FF magenta, but terrain/water tiles should generally fill the cells.
